# AppLixir

AppLixir is a web-first rewarded video advertising platform for HTML5 games and web apps.
Publishers earn $4–15 CPM. Users watch a short video in exchange for an in-game reward.

## SDK

Current version: v6.1.0
CDN URL: https://cdn.applixir.com/applixir.app.v6.1.0.js
Exposes three globals on window: initializeAndOpenPlayer(options), Application(options), and preloadAd(options).

DEPRECATED (do not use): applixir.sdk2.1m.js, invokeApplixirVideoUnit(), zoneId/devId/gameId params

## Integration (3 steps)

Step 1: Add div container
<div id="applixir-ad-container"></div>

Step 2: Load SDK script
<script type="text/javascript" src="https://cdn.applixir.com/applixir.app.v6.1.0.js"></script>

Step 3: Initialize and call on user action
const options = {
  apiKey: "YOUR-API-KEY",
  injectionElementId: "applixir-ad-container",
  adStatusCallbackFn: (status) => { if (status.type === "complete") grantReward(); },
  adErrorCallbackFn: (error) => { console.error(error.getError().data); },
};
initializeAndOpenPlayer(options);

## adStatusCallbackFn — receives a STATUS OBJECT, not a string

The callback argument is an object: { type, ad?, error? }. ALWAYS read status.type.
Comparing status to a string (e.g. status === "ad-watched") is WRONG and will never match.

status.type is one of:
- "loaded" → ad data available
- "started" → playback started
- "firstQuartile" / "midpoint" / "thirdQuartile" → playback progress
- "complete" → user watched the full ad → GRANT REWARD
- "allAdsCompleted" → fires at the end of ANY ad AND when no ad was available → do NOT grant; use to clean up
- "click" → ad clicked
- "paused" → ad paused
- "skipped" → user skipped → do NOT grant reward
- "manuallyEnded" → user closed the ad early → do NOT grant reward
- "consentDeclined" → user declined personalized-ads consent → do NOT grant reward

GRANT REWARD ONLY when status.type === "complete".

## adErrorCallbackFn

error.getError().data contains error details ({ type, errorCode, errorMessage, ... }).

## Application Class (advanced)

const app = new Application(options);
window.onload = () => { app.initialize(); };
// then call app.openPlayer() on user action

## Faster Ad Loading — preloadAd(options) (optional, for fast reveal)

By default initializeAndOpenPlayer() runs the ad auction + video request AFTER the click,
so the user waits ~1.5-3.5s on a spinner. preloadAd() splits that: it runs the auction +
video request ahead of time (on a high-intent signal), and the click only reveals the
already-loaded ad in ~100-300ms. Same options object as initializeAndOpenPlayer.

preloadAd(options) is async and returns a handle { show }. Call show() from inside the
click/tap handler (keeps the user-gesture chain — required on iOS/Safari).

let adHandle = null;
async function loadAd() {
  try { adHandle = await preloadAd(options); }   // runs auction + video request now
  catch (e) { adHandle = null; }                 // no-fill / error — fall back on click
}
loadAd();                                        // call on a high-intent signal, NOT page load
btn.addEventListener("click", async () => {
  if (adHandle) { await adHandle.show(); adHandle = null; loadAd(); } // reveal, then re-preload
  else initializeAndOpenPlayer(options);         // fallback when preload missing/failed
});

Rules:
- Preload on a high-intent signal (reward modal mount, level complete, "out of coins"), NOT on page load — page-load preload wastes ad requests on non-clickers.
- Each handle is single-use. Re-call preloadAd() after each show() for the next click.
- ALWAYS keep an on-click initializeAndOpenPlayer() fallback (~10-30% of preloads no-fill; Incognito / blocked 3p-cookies can fail silently).
- Prebid video bids TTL after ~5 min; if the gap between preloadAd() and show() may exceed that, refresh on a timer or re-preload on the next intent signal.
- For an always-visible "Watch Ad" button: preloadAd() when it first renders, refresh every ~4 min, and refresh on visibilitychange→visible (not while backgrounded).

## Requirements

- Must be triggered by explicit user interaction (click/tap) — no autoplay
- Register your exact site URL in the AppLixir dashboard
- Add ads.txt entries from dashboard to your domain root
- Works with: HTML5, Phaser, PixiJS, Unity WebGL, Cocos, React, Vue, vanilla JS

## React (web)

No npm package — load the CDN script (in index.html or injected via the hook).
adStatusCallbackFn still receives the status OBJECT; reward on status.type === "complete".
Example hook (JS + TS) + component: /examples/react/ in this repo.
Full guide: https://support.applixir.com/applixir-integration/react-integration/

## React Native (IMPORTANT)

AppLixir is a browser SDK; in React Native you run it inside react-native-webview.
The WebView MUST load a real https:// URL on your registered domain via source={{ uri }}.
NEVER use source={{ html: ... }} or a file:// URL: that gives the page origin "null", which
breaks the consent postMessage handshake ("Invalid target origin 'null'") and makes the ad
server return "AdError 303: No Ads" because it cannot match your registered domain.
Pattern: host an ad page (loads SDK, postMessage bridge) on your domain; point the WebView at it.
Example (hosted page + RN screen): /examples/react-native/ in this repo.
Full guide: https://support.applixir.com/applixir-integration/react-integration/step-4-react-native

## Unity WebGL

Use ApplixirWebGL.PlayVideo(callback). Positive result: PlayVideoResult.ADWatched.

## Server-Side Callback (optional, recommended)

Configure in dashboard → Callbacks. AppLixir POSTs a signed HTTPS request to your endpoint
after server-side validation of each completed ad. This is the source of truth for granting
persistent rewards; treat the client-side "complete" as optimistic UI only.

## Links

Sign up: https://client.applixir.com/register
Dashboard: https://client.applixir.com
Documentation: https://support.applixir.com
Integration examples: https://github.com/applixirinc/applixir-integration
Support: support@applixir.com
